headline: Chip Stocks Ignite Monday Rally as Breadth Holds Narrow Edge
body_html:
Midday Monday, the semiconductor complex is providing the clearest directional pull in an otherwise divided market. With 205 advancers against 188 decliners across our 396-name universe, the session is far from a broad surge — but the magnitude of gains in chip-related names is doing heavy lifting. Connectivity specialist Credo Technology leads all movers with an advance exceeding 13%, while AMD has surged nearly 9% and Arista Networks has climbed more than 8%, dragging the Semiconductors sector to a gain of roughly 4.8% — the sharpest sector reading on either side of the ledger.

Data-center infrastructure is participating alongside chips: Vertiv Holdings is up 7.5% intraday, reinforcing a theme of capital flowing toward AI-adjacent hardware and power. Bloom Energy adds another dimension, rising nearly 9% as fuel-cell and alternative-energy names catch a bid. Energy as a whole is modestly positive, aided by oil prices holding near levels last seen before the Iran conflict escalated, with global supply chains absorbing the shock even as depleted reserves remain a longer-term concern.

The soft underbelly of today's session sits in consumer and materials names. Auto-parts retailers O'Reilly Automotive and AutoZone have shed 6.5% and 5.5% respectively, while specialty chemicals name Solstice Advanced Materials has collapsed nearly 15% — the session's starkest single-stock loss. Homebuilders are the weakest sector at –1.97%. Heading into the close, watch whether semiconductor momentum broadens or fades as macro headlines out of the Middle East and NATO continue to compete for attention.

briefs.headline: Lockheed Martin Moves to Acquire Ultra Maritime in $3.45 Billion Deal
briefs.body: Lockheed Martin has agreed to purchase maritime defense technology firm Ultra Maritime for $3.45 billion, a transaction that underscores accelerating consolidation across the defense sector. The deal arrives as investors reconsider how to value defense and electronic-warfare capabilities amid evolving geopolitical tensions globally.

briefs.headline: Suez Canal Trade Route Reopens as Major Shippers Return
briefs.body: Shipping giants Maersk and Hapag-Lloyd have resumed transiting the Suez Canal, signaling growing confidence that the waterway is safe for commercial traffic. The development could ease global freight costs and supply-chain pressures that built during the extended period of Red Sea disruptions.

briefs.headline: UAE Crude Output Approaches Record Levels After OPEC Departure
briefs.body: The United Arab Emirates is pumping crude at volumes close to an all-time high following its exit from the OPEC production framework, according to sources familiar with the matter. The surge in Gulf supply is helping the world offset the historic loss of Iranian barrels, though analysts caution that strategic petroleum reserves remain stretched.

briefs.headline: Trump Set to Ring Opening Bell With Corporate Leaders in Unprecedented White House Ceremony
briefs.body: President Trump is scheduled to preside over a market-open ceremony at the White House alongside a roster of prominent chief executives, a first-of-its-kind event blending presidential pageantry with financial markets. The gathering drew wide attention ahead of Monday's session open.

briefs.headline: Hamas Dissolves Gaza Government in Bid to Revive Stalled Peace Process
briefs.body: Hamas announced it is disbanding the governing authority it maintained in Gaza and signaled a push to accelerate negotiations over a stalled peace framework. The move represents a notable political shift that diplomats say could open new channels for a ceasefire agreement.

briefs.headline: SpaceX President Plans Stock Donation to Trump Administration Accounts
briefs.body: Gwynne Shotwell, president of SpaceX, has announced plans to donate company stock to accounts associated with the Trump administration's new savings vehicle program. The pledge draws one of Silicon Valley's most prominent aerospace executives into the orbit of the administration's financial initiatives.
